persona: new student deciding whether to pay
state: fresh install, normal mode, onboarding complete, not Plus
expected: no forced paywall appears before value
student can scan/import or try demo
Plus is introduced after value or at advanced feature gates
failureMeans: The user is asked to pay before seeing the semester-plan value.

state: not Plus with 2 classes, 12 homework items, or 1 used import
trigger: adds more work, imports another syllabus, or opens advanced automation
expected: CTA explains expanded classes/homework/scans and automation
failureMeans: Free feels either punitive too early or unbounded enough to remove Plus leverage.

state: not Plus after onboarding or demo
trigger: looks at bottom navigation and command tiles
expected: free tab bar exposes Today, Scan, Plan, Classes, Widgets
focus, grades, advanced widgets, reminders, and calendar sync route to Plus
failureMeans: Free users cannot reach the core loop or see confusing fake premium surfaces.

evidence: storage does not fall back to defaults
failureMeans: The app looks fake and violates student trust.

state: user with syllabus parse result and import allowance
failureMeans: The parser wedge does not turn into a daily plan.

expected: native widgets use reviewed planner data
daily widget stack explains student value
failureMeans: Fake controls create trust debt and App Store review risk.

trigger: import applied, assignment completed, focus completed, or widget saved
failureMeans: Review prompt annoys users before value.

state: app icon visible at small size
failureMeans: The icon fights the premium academic positioning.
